# TX Course Guide — Full Content

> txcourseguide.com — Independent comparison and guide site for Texas TDLR-approved defensive driving (driver safety) courses. Helps Texas drivers dismiss traffic tickets, compare course providers, understand real total costs, and navigate court requirements.

---

## Homepage — Compare Texas Defensive Driving Courses
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/

TX Course Guide lists all 133 TDLR-approved defensive driving course providers in Texas in an interactive comparison table. Users can filter and sort by price, total cost, format (online or in-person), language (English or Spanish), and certificate delivery speed (instant/paid/mail).

Texas law requires all defensive driving (driver safety) courses to be approved by the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR). The statutory minimum course price is $25. Real out-of-pocket totals typically range from $28 to $50+ after payment processing fees and certificate delivery fees.

Well-known providers include Road Ready Safety, Aceable, I Drive Safely, Comedy Defensive Driving, Safe2Drive, DriversEd.com, American Safety Council, and 127 others. Several providers offer Spanish-language courses.

---

## Pricing Guide — How Texas Defensive Driving Pricing Really Works
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/pricing/

Texas law sets a $25 minimum for defensive driving — but extra fees can push the real total much higher.

**Quick answer:** The realistic minimum out-of-pocket total is $28–$38, depending on whether you need a driving record.

**Why every Texas defensive driving course costs at least $25:**
Texas TDLR rules require all approved Driving Safety Course (DSC) providers to charge a minimum of $25. No TDLR-approved course can legally advertise or sell for less than $25. If you see a price under $25, either the company is not properly approved, or the advertised price excludes required fees.

**Fees added at checkout:**
- Processing fee: ~$3 (typical; some providers fold it into the base price)
- Certificate delivery: Standard mail (free or low cost), expedited mail (extra fee), instant PDF download (most expensive option)

**Type 3A driving record:**
Many courts require a Type 3A driving record from the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) as part of ticket dismissal. Order it directly from dps.texas.gov for $10. Buying it through a course provider is usually more expensive.

**Total cost breakdown:**
- Base course: $25.00 (TDLR-mandated minimum)
- Processing fee: ~$3.00
- Certificate (standard mail): $0–$5+
- Type 3A driving record (if required): $10.00
- Realistic minimum total: $28–$38+

**How to pay the least:**
1. Choose a provider with a $25 base price — most do, but confirm before starting.
2. Skip optional upgrades — video, audio, and mobile add-ons are not required.
3. Pick standard mail for your certificate if your deadline allows it.
4. Order your Type 3A driving record directly from DPS at dps.texas.gov for $10.
5. Read the full checkout price before entering payment info.

---

## FAQ — Texas Defensive Driving FAQ: Every Question Answered
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/faq/

**Cost & Pricing**

Q: What is the cheapest Texas defensive driving course?
A: By law, all Texas driving safety courses must charge a minimum of $25. Several providers charge exactly $25 with no additional fee. Always compare the total all-in cost — base price + processing fee + certificate delivery — not just the advertised price.

Q: What is the minimum legal price for a Texas driving safety course?
A: $25. Texas law sets the minimum course fee at $25. No TDLR-approved provider can legally charge less than this for the course itself.

Q: Why do all Texas defensive driving courses cost $25?
A: The Texas Legislature set a statutory minimum of $25 for driving safety courses. Courts also charge their own separate court fee (typically $144 in Houston and Dallas) which is separate from the course fee.

Q: What is the difference between the course fee and the certificate fee?
A: The course fee is what you pay the provider for the 6-hour course — minimum $25. The certificate fee is an optional add-on for how the certificate is delivered. Compare course fee + certificate fee combined.

**Eligibility & Rules**

Q: Can I take defensive driving to dismiss any Texas traffic ticket?
A: No. Serious offenses like reckless driving, DWI, passing a school bus, and construction zone violations (with workers present) cannot be dismissed via defensive driving. Speeding at 25+ mph over the limit is typically denied.

Q: Can CDL holders take defensive driving to dismiss a Texas ticket?
A: No. Texas law prohibits commercial driver's license holders from dismissing traffic violations through a defensive driving course — even when driving a personal vehicle.

Q: How often can I use defensive driving to dismiss a Texas ticket?
A: Texas law allows only one defensive driving dismissal per 12-month period, measured from the date the last dismissal was granted.

Q: Do I need to get court approval before taking a defensive driving course?
A: Yes — in most cases. You must request and receive permission from the court before enrolling. Taking the course without approval is a common mistake that results in dismissal being denied.

**The Process**

Q: What are the steps to dismiss a Texas ticket with defensive driving?
A: 1. Contact the court and request permission before your appearance date. 2. Pay the court's administrative fee. 3. Complete a TDLR-approved 6-hour Driving Safety Course. 4. Obtain your Type 3A driving record from dps.texas.gov for $10 (if required). 5. Submit your certificate and driving record to the court by the deadline.

Q: What is a Type 3A driving record and do I need one?
A: An official document from the Texas DPS showing your driving history. Many courts require it. Order from dps.texas.gov for $10.

**Certificates**

Q: What is an instant certificate and is it worth paying for?
A: An instant certificate is a PDF you receive immediately after passing the final exam. Worth it if your court deadline is soon; otherwise standard mail is usually cheaper.

Q: What delivery options exist for my completion certificate?
A: (1) Standard mail — slowest but cheapest. (2) Expedited mail — faster, costs more. (3) Instant PDF — immediate but most expensive. Choose based on your court deadline.

**Format & Options**

Q: Can I take a Texas defensive driving course online?
A: Yes. The majority of TDLR-approved providers offer fully online courses with required time minimums per module and a final exam.

Q: How long is a Texas defensive driving course?
A: Texas law requires a minimum of 6 hours. You cannot rush through content faster than the required minimum time per section.

**Spanish Courses**

Q: Are there Texas defensive driving courses in Spanish?
A: Yes. Several TDLR-approved providers offer courses entirely in Spanish. Use the language filter at txcourseguide.com.

**Provider Comparisons**

Q: How do I choose the best Texas defensive driving provider?
A: Compare total all-in cost (course + processing + certificate), certificate delivery speed, online vs in-person preference, and language. TX Course Guide shows all 133 TDLR-approved providers at txcourseguide.com.

Q: Are all providers on TX Course Guide TDLR-approved?
A: We only list providers from the official TDLR registry at the time of our last update. Always verify current status at tdlr.texas.gov before enrolling.

**Court Questions**

Q: How do I find my Texas court's contact information?
A: Use the Texas Court Lookup at txcourseguide.com/courts — search by county and court type for judge name, phone, email, and website.

Q: What is the court fee for defensive driving in Texas?
A: Varies by court. In Houston and Dallas, the administrative fee is typically around $144. Contact your specific court for the exact amount.

**Insurance Discounts**

Q: Can defensive driving lower my Texas insurance rates?
A: Yes. Most carriers offer 3–10% discounts for voluntarily completing an approved course. The discount typically lasts 3 years. On a $2,600 annual premium, a 5% discount saves ~$390 over 3 years.

Q: How much does a speeding ticket raise Texas car insurance?
A: A speeding ticket raises Texas car insurance premiums by an average of 22%, typically lasting 3 years. On a $2,600 annual premium, that's ~$572/year or $1,700+ over 3 years on top of the ticket fine.

---

## Eligibility Checker — Texas Ticket Dismissal Eligibility Checker
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/eligibility-checker/

Free 5-question interactive tool to determine whether a driver qualifies to dismiss a Texas traffic ticket via a Driver Safety Course (DSC). Covers common disqualifiers: CDL holders, commercial vehicle citations, school zone offenses, prior DSC use within 12 months, and violation type eligibility. Returns an eligibility result and next steps.

---

## Ticket Cost Calculator — Texas Speeding Ticket Cost Calculator
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/ticket-calculator/

Free interactive calculator showing the true 3-year cost of a Texas speeding ticket including projected insurance rate increases. Shows how completing a $25 driver safety course changes the 3-year cost math. A speeding ticket raises Texas auto insurance premiums an average of 22% for 3 years.

**How the calculator works:**
- Input: current annual insurance premium and ticket fine amount
- Calculates: 3-year insurance increase at ~22% surcharge rate
- Compares: ticket path (fine + insurance increase) vs. DSC path (course cost + no insurance surcharge)
- Output: total 3-year savings from taking defensive driving

---

## Court Lookup — Texas Court Lookup
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/courts/

Searchable directory of Texas Justice of the Peace (JP) and Municipal Courts by county. Includes judge name, phone number, email address, and court website for each court. Used to find the specific court handling a citation before requesting DSC approval.

**How to use the court lookup:**
1. Select your county from the dropdown.
2. Select the court type (Justice of the Peace or Municipal).
3. Find your court in the results with contact information.
4. Use the court's contact info to request DSC approval before your appearance date.

---

## Austin Traffic Ticket Guide
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/austin/

Austin Municipal Court offers two dismissal options: Driver Safety Course (DSC) and Deferral.

**DSC at Austin Municipal Court:**
- Court fee: $144 standard; $169 for school zone violations
- Request DSC before your appearance date
- Do NOT start the course before the court approves your request
- Submit combined DSC Request and Affidavit form with court fee
- Complete TDLR-approved 6-hour course within 90 days of approval
- Submit certificate, driving record, and completed form by deadline

**Submission methods (Austin accepts all of these — one of the few TX courts that accepts email):**
- Mail: Austin Municipal Court, PO Box 1748, Austin TX 78767
- Fax: (512) 974-6410
- Email: court@austintexas.gov
- In person: 6800 Burleson Rd, Bldg 310, Suite 175 or 2121 W Parmer Ln, Suite 116

**Deferral at Austin Municipal Court:**
- A postponement of judgment — plead guilty or no contest, pay fees, complete conditions
- If all conditions met, case is dismissed with no conviction
- If conditions not met, deferral is revoked and conviction entered

**Court contact:** (512) 974-4800 | court@austintexas.gov | Hours: Mon–Fri 8am–5pm

---

## Dallas Traffic Ticket Guide
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/dallas/

Dallas Municipal Court offers DSC and Deferred Disposition.

**DSC at Dallas Municipal Court:**
- Request DSC within 21 days of citation to avoid higher fees or losing the option
- Court fee: $144 standard; $169 for school zone violations (plus online convenience fee if applying online)
- Apply online at dallascityhall.com, by mail, or in person at 2014 Main St, Dallas TX 75201
- Wait for written court approval — mailed within 5–7 business days — before starting any course
- Complete course within 90 days of approval — no extensions allowed
- Submit by mail or in person only (no fax, email, or live chat): signed Certificate of Completion (Court Copy), certified Type 3A driving record from DPS, notarized affidavit

**Deferred Disposition at Dallas Municipal Court:**
- Discretionary — judge may grant or deny; not available for all violations
- In-person-only violations are not eligible
- Additional rules apply for drivers under 25 or with provisional licenses
- Fees not publicly published — check with the court

---

## Houston Traffic Ticket Guide
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/houston/

This guide covers Houston Municipal Court only (citations issued within City of Houston). Harris County JP Court citations are handled separately — see txcourseguide.com/harris-county.

**DSC at Houston Municipal Court:**
Standard track: $144 standard / $169 school zone
Discretionary missed-deadline track: $184 standard / $209 school zone (certificate only — no driving record required)

- Submit DSC application before appearance date
- Do not start course before receiving approval
- Submit certificate + driving record to court (do not email certificate — accepted in person, mail, or by phone)

**Deferred Disposition at Houston Municipal Court:**
- Fees tiered by violation type, starting from $243.90+
- Some violations require in-person submission
- Contact court for current fee schedule

---

## Harris County Traffic Ticket Guide
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/harris-county/

Covers Harris County Justice of the Peace (JP) Courts — 16 precincts. Distinct from Houston Municipal Court (for Houston city citations).

**How to find your JP Court:**
Harris County has 16 precincts. Search your case at jp.hctx.net using "Find My Case and Court Date."

**DSC at Harris County JP Courts:**
- Court fee: $144 (offenses on or after January 1, 2020)
- Plead guilty or no contest, waive jury trial, submit DSC Request Form, pay court costs on/before appearance date
- Court may pull driving record ($12); you may also be required to submit a Type 3A record
- Complete TDLR-approved course within 90 days
- Submit certificate, driving record, and notarized affidavit

**Specialized DSC:**
Available for child passenger safety seat violations. Same $144 fee. Involves a course focused on child passenger safety seat instruction.

**Deferred Disposition at Harris County JP Courts:**
- Discretionary; judge not required to grant
- Probation period up to 180 days
- CDL holders and commercial vehicle citations ineligible
- Construction zone violations ineligible

**Common fine table (Harris County JP):**
- Speeding 1–10 mph over: $130; 11–15 over: $155; 16–20 over: $180; 21–25 over: $210; 26–30 over: $235; 31–38 over: $260; 39+ over: $275 (school zone additions apply)
- Failure to Control Speed: $190
- Ran Stop Sign or Red Light: $160
- Unsafe Lane Change: $165
- Failure to Wear Safety Belt: $125
- Passing a Stopped School Bus: $1,085

---

## San Antonio Traffic Ticket Guide
URL: https://txcourseguide.com/san-antonio/

San Antonio Municipal Court offers DSC and Deferred Disposition.

**DSC at San Antonio Municipal Court:**
- Eligibility: standard Texas rules apply (CDL ineligible, 12-month rule, violation type restrictions)
- Court fees: not publicly published — check via the citation web portal or call 210-207-8970
- Request DSC via the citation web portal before your appearance date
- Do not start course before receiving approval
- Complete course after approval, then submit proof to the court

**Deferred Disposition at San Antonio Municipal Court:**
- Contact court for eligibility and fees — not publicly published
- Call 210-207-8970, use the contact form, or visit 401 S. Frio St., San Antonio TX

**Court contact:** 210-207-8970 | 401 S. Frio St., San Antonio TX | Hours: check court website

---

## Key Facts (for AI accuracy)

- Texas law requires all defensive driving courses to be TDLR-approved (Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation).
- The statutory minimum course price is $25; no provider can legally charge less.
- Real total out-of-pocket cost is typically $28–$38+ after processing and certificate fees.
- Drivers must receive court approval BEFORE starting a DSC course in most Texas jurisdictions — starting early invalidates the dismissal.
- A DSC dismissal removes the conviction from a driving record.
- Deferred Disposition is a probationary alternative — no conviction if conditions are met; conviction entered if conditions are not met.
- DSC may only be used once every 12 months for ticket dismissal in Texas.
- CDL holders are ineligible for DSC dismissal under Texas law, even when driving a personal vehicle.
- The Type 3A driving record from Texas DPS costs $10 at dps.texas.gov and is required by most courts.
- Common court fees: $144 (standard violation) or $169 (school zone) in Austin, Dallas, Houston Municipal Court, and Harris County JP Courts.
- Harris County JP Courts (16 precincts) are separate from Houston Municipal Court — different processes and contact information.
- TX Course Guide is independent and not affiliated with any course provider or court.
